---
title: "Hp, exp design and expected results"
author: 
    name: Michele Garbelotto
    email: garbelotto.michele@gmail.com
bibliography: references.bib
csl: apa.csl
format:
  revealjs:
    self-contained: true
    slide-number: c/t
    show-slide-number: all
    progress: false
    center: false
    theme: simple
    fontsize: 24.5px
    margin: 0.20
    width: 1000
    height: 550
    scrollable: false
    transition-speed: slow
    transition: none
execute:
  echo: false
---

# Hypothesis

> People experience less social comparison in the loss domain, in contrast to the gain domain

. . .

> If loss domain, then less social comparison

. . .

An exploratory question: is there a difference in the direction of comparison between the two domains?

# Experimental design
:::: {.columns}

::: {.column}

1. True experiment
2. within-subjects design
3. blocks
    1. loss domain
        1. distance
        2. magnitude
    2. gain domain
        1. distance
        2. magnitude

:::

::: {.column}

4. IV
    1. domain
    2. distance
    3. magnitude
    4. roulette outcome (best vs worst)
5. DV
    1. sample choice
    2. sample amount 
    
:::

::::

# Expected results

> There will be a lower average of sample choice and sample amount in the loss domain in comparison to the gain domain
